iterations.label: telegram-local-ingest-mvp
iterations.goal: Build a Telegram Local Bot API Server based local ingest worker that writes immutable Obsidian raw bundles and triggers a constrained LLM wiki ingest adapter.
iterations.summary: Completed. Sprint 0 seeded the downstream project from vibe-doctor. Sprint 1 added the Telegram Local Bot API baseline, startup checks, tests, and operator setup docs. Sprint 2 added SQLite schema, repository functions, state transitions, retry eligibility, and event history. Sprint 3 added Telegram update parsing, command parsing, allowlist checks, durable offset polling, and /ingest job creation. Sprint 4 added controlled Telegram file import into runtime staging/archive with hashing, duplicate detection, max size policy, and path safety. Sprint 5 added immutable Obsidian raw bundle writing with manifest/source/log files and finalized overwrite protection. Sprint 6 added RTZR STT auth, submit/poll, transcript artifacts, 429 backoff, and ffmpeg helpers. Sprint 7 added a protected wiki ingest adapter command boundary. Sprint 8 added Telegram status/retry/cancel, notification/report text, and operator guidance.

iterations.label: llmwiki-foundation
iterations.goal: Build the LLMwiki foundation for telegram-local-ingest by defining immutable raw evidence, deterministic canonical text projections, wiki_inputs schema, disciplined ingest rules, and retention/reconcile boundaries.
iterations.summary: Started through vibe-iterate. Carryover: Sprint 12 is passed; STT transcript downloads now render DOCX while raw transcript Markdown remains canonical. New decision: rendered user deliverables are not wiki raw. LLMwiki reads raw bundle source.md, manifest.yaml, and declared canonical wiki_inputs only. Runtime outputs, _translated files, overlay PDFs, and transcript DOCX files are convenience outputs, not source authority. Existing Sprint 13 reconcile/retention remains in scope but is scheduled after the wiki raw/input contract so reconcile can understand wiki_inputs and citations.
iterations.carryoverSummary: Prior work provides Telegram capture, immutable raw bundle writing, STT, deterministic preprocessing, agent postprocess outputs, and wiki adapter boundaries. The unresolved LLMwiki gap is that canonical DOCX/PDF/EML/image preprocessing currently happens after raw finalization and may remain runtime-only; iter-2 makes canonical text durable in raw/** before wiki ingest.
openRisks: OCR and STT artifacts may contain recognition errors; wiki schema must mark confidence/source basis.
Large documents require chunking/read-order policy to avoid wasting tokens.
Existing runtime output files must remain excluded from wiki source authority even when they are easier for humans to read.
Manual raw/wiki deletion still needs deterministic reconcile and tombstone state.
